wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the EcoGrid project overview section focused on introducing the sustainable energy management platform. Explain its purpose as a comprehensive solution for real-time energy monitoring, AI-powered forecasting, and peer-to-peer energy trading through blockchain technology. Document the multi-layered architecture combining React frontend, Node.js backend, Python machine learning service, and Solidity smart contracts. Include the technology stack overview covering React.js, Node.js, Python Flask, Solidity, MongoDB, and Socket.IO. Provide an architectural diagram showing the relationship between frontend, backend, blockchain, and ML components. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and explain how the platform addresses sustainable energy challenges through innovative technology integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Architecture & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API architecture documentation for the Node.js/Express backend server. Document the server initialization, middleware configuration, and routing structure. Explain the RESTful API design principles, request/response patterns, and error handling strategies. Detail the database connection setup with MongoDB using Mongoose ODM, including connection pooling and error recovery. Cover the Socket.IO integration for real-time communication and event broadcasting. Document environment configuration, CORS settings, and security headers. Include server startup procedures, health checks, and monitoring setup. Address performance optimization techniques, request timeout configuration, and resource cleanup strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application architecture. Explain the component hierarchy, routing structure using React Router, and state management with Context API. Document the Vite build configuration and development setup. Detail the application entry point, main component structure, and provider wrappers. Cover component composition patterns, prop drilling solutions, and performance optimization techniques. Include the authentication context implementation for centralized state management. Address the build process, environment configurations, and development workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the EcoGrid platform focused on helping developers quickly set up and run the complete application locally. Document system prerequisites including Node.js, Python, MongoDB, and MetaMask wallet requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for frontend, backend, blockchain, and machine learning components. Include environment variable configuration for each service with detailed explanations of required settings. Document the development server startup sequence and port configurations. Explain how to connect to Polygon testnet and configure wallet integration. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to confirm successful installation. Provide guidance on accessing different application modules through browser URLs and initial user workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Architecture and Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the XGBoost model architecture and implementation. Explain the model's decision tree ensemble structure, hyperparameter configuration, and feature importance analysis. Document the training methodology including dataset preparation, cross-validation strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Detail the model's input features, target variables, and preprocessing requirements. Include the model serialization format, version management, and deployment considerations. Provide technical specifications for model shapes, dimensions, and memory requirements. Document the model's accuracy metrics, precision-recall trade-offs, and confidence interval calculations. Address model interpretability through SHAP values and feature contribution analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: MongoDB Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive MongoDB schema design documentation covering all collection schemas in the EcoGrid application. Detail each model's field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business constraints. Document the Users schema with authentication fields, UserProfile schema for energy consumption tracking, EnergyData schema for real-time monitoring, EnergyListing schema for marketplace functionality, Transaction schema for trading records, BlogPost schema for community features, and ResetCode schema for password recovery. Include field-level documentation explaining purpose, validation requirements, and data format specifications. Provide examples of typical document structures and explain the rationale behind each field choice. Address schema evolution considerations and backward compatibility strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Smart Contracts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive smart contract documentation covering the EnergyToken ERC20 implementation, EnergyExchange marketplace contract, and EnergyAMM automated market maker. Detail the EnergyToken contract architecture including dynamic pricing mechanism, supply management, and ownership controls. Explain the EnergyExchange contract functionality for peer-to-peer energy trading with order matching and settlement logic. Document the EnergyAMM contract for liquidity provision and automated price discovery. Include contract initialization, parameter configuration, and state management patterns. Provide detailed function signatures, event emissions, and access control mechanisms. Address security considerations including reentrancy protection, overflow/underflow prevention, and owner-only functions. Document the inheritance from OpenZeppelin contracts and custom extensions. Include practical examples of contract interactions and common usage patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development environment setup documentation. Document system requirements including Node.js, Python, MongoDB, and blockchain development tools. Explain the step-by-step installation process for each component including dependencies, version requirements, and compatibility matrices. Detail environment variable configuration for frontend (.env), backend (.env), and blockchain (.env) applications. Cover database setup with MongoDB connection strings and local development configurations. Document the development server startup process for React frontend, Node.js backend, and Python ML service. Include hot reload configuration, development scripts, and debugging setup. Explain the integration between components during development including API connectivity, real-time communication setup, and blockchain network simulation. Provide troubleshooting guides for common development environment issues and dependency conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the authentication system covering user registration, login, logout, password reset, and profile management endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and JWT token usage. Include authentication middleware implementation, session management, and role-based access control. Detail password hashing, email verification, and security headers. Provide practical examples with curl commands and SDK integration guidelines. Document error responses, validation rules, and rate limiting. Address common authentication scenarios, token refresh mechanisms, and account security features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Common Issues
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for common issues encountered during EcoGrid development and deployment. Document authentication problems including JWT token expiration, session timeout issues, and login failures with specific error messages and resolution steps. Cover environment configuration issues such as missing .env variables, incorrect API endpoints, and database connection strings. Detail frontend-specific problems including component rendering errors, state management issues, and build failures. Address backend API connectivity problems, port binding conflicts, and service startup failures. Include blockchain-related issues such as wallet connection timeouts, network switching problems, and contract deployment errors. Provide step-by-step diagnostic procedures and quick fixes for each category of issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: EnergyToken (ERC20)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed smart contract documentation for the EnergyToken ERC20 implementation. Document the token standard compliance, total supply management, and transfer mechanisms. Explain the dynamic pricing integration with energy consumption data and market conditions. Detail the minting and burning functions for supply adjustment based on grid stability and demand. Document the ownership controls, pausing mechanisms, and emergency shutdown procedures. Include comprehensive function signatures, parameter validation, and return values. Explain the integration with external price feeds and real-time energy market data. Address security measures including reentrancy guards, overflow/underflow protection, and access control patterns. Provide practical examples of token transfers, approvals, and supply adjustments. Document the contract's role in the broader EcoGrid ecosystem and its relationship with EnergyExchange and EnergyAMM contracts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for EcoGrid development environment setup. Include prerequisites covering Node.js, Python, MongoDB, and blockchain development tools. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for frontend React application, backend Node.js server, Python Flask ML service, and blockchain contracts. Document environment variable configuration for each component including API URLs, database connections, and blockchain network settings. Include verification steps to ensure all services are running correctly. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and dependency conflicts. Include first-run tutorial covering application startup sequence and initial user onboarding flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed authentication system documentation covering JWT-based user authentication and authorization. Document the complete authentication flow from registration to login, including password hashing, token generation, and session management. Explain the JWT middleware implementation for protecting routes and validating user permissions. Detail the user registration process with email verification, profile creation, and role assignment. Cover the login/logout mechanisms, token refresh strategies, and session persistence. Document the user profile management system including data validation, update operations, and security measures. Include error handling for authentication failures, account lockout policies, and security best practices. Provide code examples for client-side authentication integration and token storage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the authentication system implementation. Explain JWT token management, session persistence, and protected route handling. Document the authentication context provider, login/logout functionality, and user state management. Cover the registration process, password validation, and email verification workflows. Detail the forgot password functionality and reset token handling. Include error handling strategies, loading states, and user feedback mechanisms. Address security considerations for token storage and transmission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for EcoGrid platform focused on the complete technical foundation and component relationships. Document the frontend stack including React 19.0.0, Vite build system, Tailwind CSS, and modern JavaScript features. Explain the backend architecture using Node.js with Express 4.21.2, Socket.IO 4.8.3, and Mongoose 8.11.0 for MongoDB integration. Detail the machine learning stack with Python Flask 2.3.3, XGBoost 2.0.0, Pandas 2.1.0, and NumPy for energy forecasting. Document the blockchain technology stack including Hardhat 2.22.19, OpenZeppelin 5.2.0, Ethers.js 6.13.5, and Polygon testnet integration. Explain the real-time communication system using Socket.IO for live energy data streaming. Include dependency management strategies, version compatibility requirements, and integration patterns between different technology layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Flask API Integration and Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Flask API implementation and RESTful endpoints. Document all API endpoints including prediction requests, model information retrieval, and health checks. Detail request/response schemas, parameter validation, and error handling mechanisms. Explain the Flask application configuration, routing setup, and middleware integration. Include authentication methods, rate limiting, and security considerations. Document the API's integration with the main application through HTTP requests and response processing. Provide practical examples of API usage with cURL commands and Python requests. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation policies. Include performance benchmarks, latency measurements, and scalability considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Relationships and Associations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for data relationships and associations between MongoDB collections in the EcoGrid platform. Explain the one-to-one relationship between Users and UserProfiles, the one-to-many relationships between Users and EnergyListings/Transactions, and the many-to-many relationship facilitated by the Transaction schema for EnergyListings. Document foreign key references, embedded document patterns, and hybrid approaches used throughout the schema design. Include relationship mapping examples showing how data flows between collections during CRUD operations. Explain referential integrity constraints and cascading operations. Provide query patterns for joining related data and performance implications of different relationship strategies. Address data consistency challenges and eventual consistency patterns used in the distributed system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed deployment and configuration documentation for the blockchain system. Document the Hardhat configuration setup including network parameters, compiler settings, and artifact generation. Explain the deployment script implementation covering contract compilation, deployment transactions, and network verification. Detail the Polygon Amoy testnet configuration including RPC endpoints, private key management, and gas optimization settings. Document environment variable configuration for development and production environments. Include step-by-step deployment procedures from local development to testnet deployment. Address contract upgrade patterns using proxy contracts and upgradeability mechanisms. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues including nonce management, gas limit problems, and network connectivity issues. Document the artifact generation process and ABI management for frontend integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed production deployment documentation covering containerization, infrastructure setup, and operational procedures. Document Docker containerization strategies for React frontend, Node.js backend, Python ML service, and MongoDB database. Cover SSL certificate configuration, reverse proxy setup with Nginx, and load balancer configuration. Document the build process for optimized React frontend including asset bundling, minification, and CDN integration. Explain database migration procedures, backup strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Include monitoring setup with logging aggregation, performance metrics collection, and alerting mechanisms. Address security considerations including firewall configuration, network segmentation, and access control policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the dashboard system covering energy monitoring, analytics, and reporting endpoints. Detail response schemas for production/consumption data, efficiency metrics, and trend analysis. Provide examples for data visualization integration and export capabilities. wiki_catalogs.name: Database Troubleshooting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed troubleshooting documentation for MongoDB database connectivity and performance issues. Include comprehensive function documentation for listing management, order placement, cancellation, and execution. Address security considerations including front-running protection, gas optimization, and access control patterns. Provide practical examples of marketplace operations, order lifecycle management, and settlement procedures. Document the contract's state management, event emissions, and integration patterns with external systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive core features documentation for EcoGrid platform focused on the primary functionality delivered to users. Explain the real-time energy dashboard featuring live monitoring of energy production and consumption, historical data visualization, and system analytics. Document the peer-to-peer energy marketplace enabling users to buy and sell excess energy, listing management, transaction processing, and blockchain settlement. Explain the blockchain integration with ERC20 token system, smart contract interactions, and Polygon testnet deployment. Include community features like blog management and user interaction systems. wiki_catalogs.name: Data Processing and Preprocessing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the data processing pipeline and preprocessing workflows. Explain the data ingestion process, cleaning procedures, and feature engineering techniques. Document the handling of time-series data including temporal feature extraction, seasonality detection, and trend analysis. Detail the normalization and scaling methods applied to input features. Include the data validation processes, outlier detection, and missing value handling strategies. Document the storage formats for processed datasets including HDF5 file structures and metadata management. Explain the data freshness requirements, update schedules, and incremental learning capabilities. Address data privacy considerations, anonymization techniques, and compliance requirements. Provide examples of data transformation workflows and preprocessing scripts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indexing Strategies and Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MongoDB indexing strategies and performance optimization in the EcoGrid application. Document compound indexes for frequently queried fields including energy data timestamps, user authentication fields, listing status filters, and transaction date ranges. Explain the rationale behind TTL indexes for temporary reset codes and automatic cleanup of old data. Include performance benchmarks and query optimization techniques for real-time energy monitoring and marketplace operations. Document index maintenance procedures, monitoring strategies for index effectiveness, and troubleshooting slow queries. Provide examples of EXPLAIN output analysis and query plan optimization. Address indexing trade-offs between write performance and read efficiency, especially for high-frequency energy data updates.

Explain the fee collection system, revenue sharing with liquidity providers, and protocol treasury mechanisms. Include comprehensive function documentation for liquidity management, swap operations, and fee calculations. Address security measures including flash loan protection, price manipulation resistance, and reserve management controls. Provide practical examples of liquidity provision, trading operations, and fee distribution. Document the mathematical foundations, state variables, and event emissions. Explain the relationship with EnergyToken and EnergyExchange contracts in the unified energy trading ecosystem.

Detail the hook-based integration patterns, state management for blockchain operations, and error handling strategies. Cover the Socket.IO integration for real-time blockchain events, transaction status tracking, and network switching. Include the polygon testnet configuration, gas estimation, and transaction fee management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for real-time communication using Socket.IO covering connection protocols, event types, and message formats. Document WebSocket connection establishment, authentication handshake, and room-based communication patterns. Detail event types for energy data streaming, market updates, and user notifications. Include message schemas for real-time data transmission, event broadcasting, and client-server communication. Document connection management, reconnection strategies, and error handling. Provide examples for integrating real-time features, handling disconnections, and optimizing bandwidth usage. Address security considerations, rate limiting for real-time events, and performance monitoring for high-frequency updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blockchain Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive blockchain integration documentation for the Solidity smart contracts and Polygon testnet deployment. Explain the EnergyToken ERC20 implementation for energy unit representation and the EnergyExchange marketplace contract for peer-to-peer trading. Document the contract deployment process using Hardhat and Polygon Amoy testnet configuration. Detail the frontend integration patterns for wallet connection, transaction signing, and event listening. Cover the gas optimization strategies and cost management for blockchain operations. Include the contract upgrade mechanisms and security considerations for production deployment. Document the transaction lifecycle from initiation to completion with proper error handling. Address the integration between blockchain state and traditional database records. Provide debugging techniques for smart contract interactions and common troubleshooting scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the UI components library. Explain the reusable component design, prop interfaces, and styling patterns using Tailwind CSS. Document the button, form, input, and select components with their variants and customization options. Detail the component composition patterns, accessibility features, and responsive design implementation. Cover the design system integration, theme configuration, and component testing strategies. Include the component usage examples, prop validation, and integration guidelines for consistent UI development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive real-time communication documentation for the Socket.IO-based live data streaming system. Explain the server-side Socket.IO configuration and client-side integration patterns. Document the event-driven architecture for energy data broadcasting, user activity streams, and market updates. Detail the authentication integration with JWT tokens for secure socket connections. Cover room-based communication patterns for targeted data distribution and user-specific notifications. Include connection management strategies, reconnection handling, and error recovery mechanisms. Document the data serialization formats and message protocols used for efficient real-time communication. Address performance optimization techniques for high-frequency data updates and large-scale user concurrency. Provide debugging tools and monitoring approaches for real-time system reliability.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation covering all public endpoints and interfaces. Document RESTful API endpoints for authentication, dashboard analytics, marketplace operations, and community features with complete request/response schemas. Include WebSocket API documentation for real-time communication with connection protocols, event types, and message formats. Detail the Flask API endpoints for energy forecasting with prediction request formats and model information retrieval. Provide authentication methods including JWT token usage and session management. Include error response codes, rate limiting information, and API versioning strategies. Document parameter validation rules, input sanitization, and security headers. Provide practical curl examples and SDK integration guidelines for third-party developers. Address API testing strategies and mock data generation for development purposes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation covering development setup, production deployment, and blockchain network configuration. Document the environment variable configuration for each component including database connections, API endpoints, and blockchain network settings. Explain the build processes for React frontend, Node.js backend, and Python ML service. Detail the containerization strategies using Docker for microservice deployment. Cover the Polygon testnet deployment process including wallet configuration and gas fee management. Include load balancing strategies, SSL certificate configuration, and reverse proxy setup. Document the CI/CD pipeline integration for automated deployment workflows. Address monitoring and logging setup for production environments. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and rollback procedures. Include scaling considerations for high-traffic scenarios and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues and their solutions across all application layers. Document authentication problems including JWT token expiration, session management issues, and login failures. Cover database connectivity issues, connection pooling problems, and data synchronization errors. Detail blockchain integration challenges including wallet connection failures, transaction submission errors, and network connectivity issues. Include real-time communication troubleshooting for Socket.IO connection problems and event delivery failures. Address performance optimization techniques for frontend rendering, backend API response times, and database query optimization. Document debugging strategies using logging frameworks, error tracking tools, and development console techniques. Provide step-by-step resolution guides for critical system failures and recovery procedures. Include preventive maintenance recommendations and monitoring setup for early issue detection.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# EcoGrid - Sustainable Energy Management Platform

## Project Introduction

EcoGrid is a comprehensive web application designed for sustainable energy management, forecasting, and marketplace trading. The platform enables users to monitor energy consumption, predict future demand using AI-powered models, and participate in peer-to-peer energy trading through blockchain technology.

### Purpose Statement
EcoGrid aims to revolutionize the way communities manage and trade renewable energy by providing an integrated platform that combines real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, and decentralized marketplace transactions.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Enable real-time energy monitoring and visualization
- Provide accurate energy demand forecasting using machine learning
- Facilitate peer-to-peer energy trading with blockchain security
- Support multiple energy sources (solar, wind, hydro, biomass)
- Create a sustainable energy ecosystem for prosumers and consumers

### Target Audience
- Homeowners with solar panels (prosumers)
- Energy consumers seeking renewable energy options
- Energy companies and utilities
- Environmental organizations and sustainability-focused communities

## Technical Architecture

The EcoGrid platform follows a modern microservices architecture with three main layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend React App] --> B[Backend Node.js API]
    B --> C[Python Flask Forecasting]
    B --> D[MongoDB Database]
    B --> E[Socket.IO Server]
    E --> F[Real-time Updates]
    G[Blockchain Network] --> H[Ethereum Smart Contracts]
    H --> I[Polygon Testnet]
    B --> H
    A --> J[Blockchain Integration]
    J --> H
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- React.js application with modern UI components
- Real-time energy data visualization
- Interactive marketplace interface
- Responsive design for mobile and desktop

**Backend Layer**
- Node.js Express server handling RESTful APIs
- Socket.IO for real-time communication
- MongoDB for data persistence
- Authentication and authorization middleware

**Machine Learning Layer**
- Python Flask API for energy forecasting
- XGBoost model for predictive analytics
- Real-time data processing and visualization

**Blockchain Layer**
- Solidity smart contracts for energy trading
- Polygon Amoy testnet deployment
- Decentralized token system (ETK)

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between models, views, and controllers
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted database operations
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time updates via Socket.IO
- **Factory Pattern**: Smart contract deployment and management
- **Context Provider Pattern**: State management across components

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant B as Backend API
    participant F as Forecasting Service
    participant DB as MongoDB
    participant BL as Blockchain
    
    U->>+B: User Request
    B->>+DB: Data Query
    DB-->>-B: Return Data
    alt Energy Forecast Request
        B->>+F: Forecast Request
        F-->>-B: Predictions
    end
    alt Blockchain Transaction
        B->>+BL: Smart Contract Call
        BL-->>-B: Transaction Receipt
    end
    B-->>-U: Response Data
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Entry Point**
- `frontend/src/main.jsx` - React application bootstrap with authentication provider
- `frontend/src/App.jsx` - Main routing component with protected routes

**Backend Entry Point**
- `backend/index.js` - Express server with Socket.IO integration and API routes
- `pythonfiles/app.py` - Flask API for machine learning predictions

**Blockchain Entry Point**
- `blockchain/scripts/deploy.js` - Smart contract deployment automation
- `blockchain/contracts/EnergyToken.sol` - ERC20 token implementation

### Core Modules

**Authentication System**
- `frontend/src/Context/AuthContext.jsx` - Centralized authentication state management
- `backend/Routes/AuthRouter.js` - User authentication and profile management
- `backend/Middlewares/Auth.js` - JWT token validation middleware

**Energy Marketplace**
- `frontend/src/frontend/Marketplace.jsx` - Main marketplace interface with real-time listings
- `backend/Routes/ListingRouter.js` - Energy listing management
- `backend/Routes/TransactionRouter.js` - Transaction processing and history

**Real-time Monitoring**
- `backend/index.js` - Socket.IO server for live energy data streaming
- `frontend/src/components/TradeModal.jsx` - Interactive trading interface

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Variables**
- `frontend/.env` - React application configuration (API URLs, feature flags)
- `backend/.env` - Node.js server configuration (database connections, JWT secrets)
- `blockchain/.env` - Blockchain deployment configuration (private keys, network settings)

**Build Configuration**
- `frontend/vite.config.js` - Vite build optimization and development server
- `backend/package.json` - Node.js dependencies and scripts
- `blockchain/hardhat.config.js` - Smart contract compilation and testing

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React 19.0.0, React Router 7.2.0 for SPA navigation
- Socket.IO client for real-time communication
- Tailwind CSS for responsive styling
- Framer Motion for smooth animations

**Backend Dependencies**
- Express 4.21.2, Socket.IO 4.8.3 for real-time features
- Mongoose 8.11.0 for MongoDB object modeling
- Bcryptjs 3.0.2 for password hashing
- JSONwebtoken 9.0.2 for authentication

**Machine Learning Dependencies**
- Flask 2.3.3, XGBoost 2.0.0 for forecasting models
- Pandas 2.1.0, NumPy 1.25.2 for data processing
- Scikit-learn 1.3.0 for model training

**Blockchain Dependencies**
- Hardhat 2.22.19 for smart contract development
- OpenZeppelin 5.2.0 for secure contract libraries
- Ethers.js 6.13.5 for blockchain interactions

### Integration Points

**API Communication**
- Frontend communicates with backend via RESTful APIs
- Real-time updates via WebSocket connections
- Machine learning predictions through separate Flask service

**Blockchain Integration**
- Frontend connects to Polygon testnet via MetaMask
- Smart contracts deployed using Hardhat framework
- Token transfers and marketplace transactions executed on-chain

**Data Flow Architecture**

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interface] --> B[React Frontend]
    B --> C[Express Backend]
    C --> D[MongoDB Database]
    C --> E[Socket.IO Server]
    C --> F[Flask Forecasting]
    F --> G[XGBoost Model]
    C --> H[Smart Contracts]
    H --> I[Polygon Testnet]
    
    E --> J[Real-time Updates]
    J --> A
    
    subgraph "Data Processing"
        G --> K[Predictive Analytics]
        K --> C
    end
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Authentication and User Management**
- Multi-factor authentication with JWT tokens
- User profile management with energy consumption tracking
- Role-based access control (prosumer, consumer, utility)

**Energy Dashboard**
- Real-time energy production and consumption monitoring
- Historical data visualization with interactive charts
- System performance analytics and optimization suggestions

**Energy Forecasting**
- AI-powered demand prediction using XGBoost algorithm
- Customizable forecast periods (daily, weekly, monthly)
- Seasonal trend analysis and weather impact assessment

**Energy Marketplace**
- Peer-to-peer energy trading platform
- Real-time listing management with dynamic pricing
- Transaction history and analytics dashboard
- Blockchain-based payment settlement

**Responsive Design**
- Mobile-first responsive interface
- Animated UI components with Framer Motion
- Intuitive navigation and user experience

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Data Streaming**
- `backend/index.js` - Socket.IO server implementation for live energy updates
- `frontend/src/frontend/Marketplace.jsx` - Dynamic listing updates and user interactions

**Machine Learning Integration**
- `pythonfiles/routes.py` - API endpoints for forecasting predictions
- `pythonfiles/model.py` - XGBoost model training and prediction logic

**Blockchain Smart Contracts**
- `blockchain/contracts/EnergyToken.sol` - ERC20 token implementation for energy trading
- `blockchain/contracts/EnergyExchange.sol` - Marketplace contract for P2P transactions

**State Management**
- `frontend/src/Context/AuthContext.jsx` - Centralized authentication and user state
- `frontend/src/hooks/useBlockchain.js` - Blockchain interaction hooks

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login/Signup
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: View Profile
    Authenticated --> Marketplace: Browse Listings
    Authenticated --> Forecasting: View Predictions
    Authenticated --> Profile: Manage Account
    
    Dashboard --> Trading: Buy/Sell Energy
    Marketplace --> Trading: Execute Transactions
    Forecasting --> Trading: Plan Energy Purchases
    
    Trading --> TransactionComplete: Successful Trade
    TransactionComplete --> Dashboard: View Results
    Trading --> TransactionFailed: Failed Trade
    TransactionFailed --> Marketplace: Retry
    
    Authenticated --> Unauthenticated: Logout
```

The EcoGrid platform represents a comprehensive solution for sustainable energy management, combining modern web technologies with blockchain innovation to create a transparent and efficient energy trading ecosystem.

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [backend/index.js](backend/index.js)
- [frontend/src/main.jsx](frontend/src/main.jsx)
- [frontend/src/App.jsx](frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [blockchain/contracts/EnergyToken.sol](blockchain/contracts/EnergyToken.sol)
- [pythonfiles/app.py](pythonfiles/app.py)
- [frontend/package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [backend/package.json](backend/package.json)
- [blockchain/package.json](blockchain/package.json)
- [pythonfiles/requirements.txt](pythonfiles/requirements.txt)
- [frontend/src/Context/AuthContext.jsx](frontend/src/Context/AuthContext.jsx)
- [blockchain/scripts/deploy.js](blockchain/scripts/deploy.js)
- [frontend/src/frontend/Marketplace.jsx](frontend/src/frontend/Marketplace.jsx)
- [backend/DB/db.js](backend/DB/db.js)
- [frontend/src/api.js](frontend/src/api.js)

</blog>
